scenario: Sentinel Bank, a digital bank with 250000 customers, deploys a Camunda 8.8 SaaS BPMN diagram that starts with an HTTP Webhook connector to receive transaction-approved callbacks from a fraud-screening vendor. After clicking Deploy in Web Modeler, the integration team opens the Webhooks tab in the properties panel to copy the URL the vendor must POST against.
question: Which URL pattern does the activated HTTP Webhook expose on a Camunda 8.8 SaaS cluster?

options.text: The URL follows http(s)://<base URL>/inbound/<webhook ID>, where <base URL> contains the region Id and cluster Id from the API tab
The URL follows http(s)://<base URL>/<contextPath>/inbound/<webhook ID>, requiring a contextPath configured in the Helm chart
The URL follows http(s)://<base URL>/connectors/<webhook ID>, with a fixed connectors segment routed to the inbound runtime
The URL follows http(s)://<base URL>/<process ID>/<webhook ID>, embedding the BPMN process ID before the webhook path segment

a.text: Correct. SaaS webhook URLs match http(s)://<base URL>/inbound/<webhook ID>, and the base URL embeds the region Id and cluster Id available from the API tab of the cluster's client credentials.

b.text: Incorrect. The <contextPath> segment only applies to self-managed installations where a contextPath is set in the Helm chart, and it must be omitted when no contextPath is configured; SaaS uses the simpler /inbound form.

c.text: Incorrect. The activated path segment is /inbound, not /connectors; the webhook runtime exposes inbound endpoints under the fixed /inbound prefix in both SaaS and self-managed deployments.

d.text: Incorrect. The URL does not embed the BPMN process ID; the path after /inbound is the webhook ID that was configured in the properties of the HTTP Webhook connector.

explanation: When the diagram is deployed, the HTTP Webhook becomes publicly available at http(s)://<base URL>/inbound/<webhook ID> in SaaS, where the base URL carries the region Id and cluster Id from the API tab. Self-managed installations add an optional Helm-configured <contextPath> segment between the base URL and /inbound.
